steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that poor lighting deters new or inexperienced cyclists but has little effect on experienced cyclists. The blank completes: "increasing the number of streetlights in a city could potentially ______". The correct answer must follow logically from those findings.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — decrease the number of experienced cyclists riding in the city.: the passage says lighting has little effect on experienced cyclists, so more lights would not decrease their numbers. ✗
  • Choice B — increase the number of new or inexperienced cyclists riding in the city.: poor lighting deters inexperienced cyclists, so better lighting removes that barrier — directly supported by the passage. ✓
  • Choice C — increase the number of experienced cyclists riding in the city.: experienced cyclists are unaffected by lighting, so more lights would not change their numbers. ✗
  • Choice D — decrease the number of new or inexperienced cyclists riding in the city.: this reverses the logic — more lights would encourage, not deter, inexperienced cyclists. ✗
That is the correct answer.
